source_rationale: A PCA plot of the markers showed that expression of mesenchymal cell-related genes decreased with time (T2 and T3), with cells forming two groups (Fig. 3B): one (upper left) overlapping with some T1 Anti-Epithelial cells with decreased mesenchymal N-cadherin (CDH2) and fibronectin (FN1) levels; the other (middle right) group was composed only of T3 cells with decreased mesenchymal vimentin (VIM) and increased epithelial E-cadherin (CDH1) levels.

A PCA plot of the markers showed that expression of mesenchymal cell-related genes decreased with time (T2 and T3), with cells forming two groups (Fig. 3B): one (upper left) overlapping with some T1 Anti-Epithelial cells with decreased mesenchymal N-cadherin (CDH2) and fibronectin (FN1) levels; the other (middle right) group was composed only of T3 cells with decreased mesenchymal vimentin (VIM) and increased epithelial E-cadherin (CDH1) levels.
Remarkably, most cells in the Anti-Epithelial group at T1 expressed high levels of proliferation- and cell cycle-related genes according to the PCA loading plot.

There was no remarkable change in the expression of drug efflux genes [28, 29] at any time point (Fig. 2C), although ABCB1 expression was slightly lower in the T3 Dormant group (S1 Appendix: Figure S5) and ABCE1 was downregulated at T2 and T3.

A PCA plot of the markers showed that expression of mesenchymal cell-related genes decreased with time (T2 and T3), with cells forming two groups (Fig. 3B): one (upper left) overlapping with some T1 Anti-Epithelial cells with decreased mesenchymal N-cadherin (CDH2) and fibronectin (FN1) levels; the other (middle right) group was composed only of T3 cells with decreased mesenchymal vimentin (VIM) and increased epithelial E-cadherin (CDH1) levels.

A PCA plot of the markers showed that expression of mesenchymal cell-related genes decreased with time (T2 and T3), with cells forming two groups (Fig. 3B): one (upper left) overlapping with some T1 Anti-Epithelial cells with decreased mesenchymal N-cadherin (CDH2) and fibronectin (FN1) levels; the other (middle right) group was composed only of T3 cells with decreased mesenchymal vimentin (VIM) and increased epithelial E-cadherin (CDH1) levels.
It seems that with time, cells differentiated into those expressing a marker for absorption cells (KRT20) and those for secretion cells (MUC2) in the digestive tract.
Among the markers for crypt base stem cells, SOX9 appeared to be the most influential; LGR5, OLFM4, and MSI1 were not substantially expressed.
